calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-27-2010 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-13-2010 Continued To Apr-27-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-23-2010 Continued To Apr-13-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jan-26-2010 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Matthew Mellen for Clarence Bryant.

Mr. Mellen had the Petition for Final Distribution with him and is going to file it today. The matter was taken off calendar.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR #2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-06-2009 Continued To Jan-26-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Matthew Mellen for Clarence Bryant.

Mr. Mellen stated that he had sent late notice to the "Taxman" and DHS, that he was still in the notice period, and that he needs to wait until January for the four months to run. The Court continued the matter to January 26, 2010 for the filing of the petition, stated that everyday thereafter if the petition was not on file sanctions at $25.00 per day would be imposed, and that it would allow a $3,000.00 preliminary distribution to his client. Mr. Mellen stated that his client was going to request $5,000.00. The Court instructed Mr. Mellen to file the petition for preliminary distribution.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Final Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-08-2009 Continued To Oct-06-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mathew Mellen for Clarence Bryant (Administrator).

Mr. Mellen stated that they are closer than ever, that they need to send notice to the tax office (State of California), that his client is in a financial hardship, and that his client has $3000.00 to live on. The Court inquired as to whether or not the creditor's claims have been paid. Mr. Mellen stated that all the creditor's claims have been paid, that DHS has been satisfied, and that he didn't notice the State of California. The Court continued the matter to October 6, 2009 and stated that if counsel petitioned the Court for preliminary distribution it would grant it.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Scott Hughes, CSR# 12365. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Final Distribution
Off Calendar Aug-25-2009 Continued To Sep-08-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Final Distribution
Off Calendar Aug-04-2009 Continued To Aug-25-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Matthew Mellen for Clarence Bryant.

Mr. Mellen requested more time stating that the he was working on the petition for final distribution, that he ran into a couple of snags and that he was communicating with Mr. Handlos' estate with regard to fees. The matter was continued to August 25, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Tamra Keen, CSR#5404. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jul-21-2009 Continued To Aug-04-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any party, counsel or interested person.

9:15 a.m.
An Order to Show Cause will be issued to the Administrator and the Attorney to appear and show cause why he/she should not be punished for contempt for failure to appear. The return date is September 1, 2009 at 9:00 a.m, in Dept. 204.

9:45 a.m.
Appearances: Robert Peiser appeared on behalf of Matthew Mellen for Clarence Bryant.

Mr. Peiser requested a two week continuance stating that they were still verifying data. The matter was continued to August 4, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for proof of filing of the Petition For Final Distribution. The Court informed Mr. Peiser that if the petition is not filed by that date, Mr. Mellen and Mr. Bryant are order to appear and explain why it has not been filed and stated that sanctions may be an option. The Court withdrew the Order To Show Cause.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Jacqueline Chan, CSR#10276. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-23-2009 Continued To Jul-21-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-09-2009 Continued To Jun-23-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Final Distribution
Off Calendar May-19-2009 Continued To Jun-09-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Matthew Mellen for and with Clarence Bryant. Norman Montalvo (Real Estate Broker).

Mr. Mellen reported that the house had been saved from foreclosure and that they were able to negotiate and pay the DHS lien. In addition, he stated that a balance of $27,245.00 was being held in the attorney/client trust account and that this was accomplished due to Mr. Montalvo's great efforts. The matter was continued to June 9, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on filing of the Petition For Final Distribution. The Court stated that it would consider a preliminary distribution for a small amount if a petition was filed.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Final Distribution
Off Calendar Apr-21-2009 Continued To May-19-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Matthew Mellen for and with Clarence Bryant. Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator ("SFPA"). Norman Montalvo (Real Estate Broker) also present.

Mr. Mellen advised the Court that they had a buyer for the property, that he needed to negotiate the medical bills with DHS and requested that Mr. Bryant be reinstated. Mr. Ferdon stated that the SFPA had never been appointed as they were not able to obtain the information needed to take over the estate. Mr. Montalvo informed the Court that he was able to stop the foreclosure and that they were ready to move forward with the sale of the property, but that there was a problem with Mr. Bryant not having the authority to proceed. The matter was continued to May 19, 2009. The issue regarding reinstatement of Mr. Bryant is being addressed by the Court.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Final Distribution
Off Calendar Mar-18-2009 Continued To Apr-21-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Janathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator ("SFPA"). Clarence Bryant in Propria Persona.

Mr. Ferdon stated that Mr. Phiezer was a mortgage broker, that he tried to reach Attorney Mr. Malen, that the foreclosure proceedings were being heard today and that Mr. Malen was attempting to get a temporary restraining order to stop the sale. In addition, he stated that there was nothing the SFPA could do, that he did not see a lien from Health Services, that Mr. Bryant had been forthcoming, that the porperty was in terrible condition and suggested a continuance for status on the foreclosure. The matter was continued to April 21, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court directed Mr. Bryant to direct Mr. Malen to appear on that date and report on the status of the foreclosure.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley, CSR 225. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/ Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy R. Lee.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Final Distribution
Off Calendar Mar-11-2009 Continued To Mar-18-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator. Clarence Bryant in Propria Persona.

Mr. Ferdon and Mr. Bryant met outside of the courtroom to discuss the issues. Upon returning, Mr. Ferdon requested a one week continuance stating that Mr. Bryant had obtained Counsel to assist him in taking action against his Cousin regarding the issue with the loan funds. The matter was continued to March 18, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court will not proceed with the issue of contempt.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Svahlin.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Final Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-25-2009 Continued To Mar-11-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator.

Mr. Ferdon stated that he was concerned about Counsel's health, that he did not receive a response from Mr. Bryant and that he did not have any information on the status of the property. Mr. Ferdon will send a letter to Mr. Bryant stating that the Court is ready to appoint the Public Administrator with the authority to sell the real property. The matter was continued to March 11, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court will notify Mr. Bryant to appear on that date.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Guitron.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Final Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-10-2009 Continued To Feb-25-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jan-27-2009 Continued To Feb-10-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator. John Murphy specially appeared for Michael Handlos for Clarence Bryant.

Mr. Murphy stated that he appeared only for the purpose of requesting a continuance due to Mr. Handlos health condition, that he was not assisting Counsel with the matter and that he did not know about Counsel's future plans. The matter was continued to February 10, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Handlos is ordered to appear. The Court requested something in writing from Mr. Handlos' Physician stating that he is medically unable to attend, the expected return date and a declaration from Mr. Handlos setting forth the plan for handling the estate. If not, the Court will proceed with the Order To Show Cause Re Contempt.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Shannon.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-23-2008 Continued To Jan-27-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Handlos for Clarence Bryant. Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA).

Clarence Bryant was not present in Court. Mr. Handlos stated that he had submitted some information over to the Public Administrator for consideration of taking over the estate. In addition, he reported that the amount of the loan was for $230,000.00 and that the last time he spoke to his Client was about two months ago. Mr. Ferdon stated that he needed an accounting before the SFPA could take over, that the only other Interested Party was the Department of Health Services (DHA) for an amount over $200,000.00 and that they had not made an appearance. In addition, he stated that he was not sure if Mr. Bryant was residing in the property. The Court directed Mr. Handlos to file a Petition For Final Distribution on waiver of account. A status hearing on filing of said petition was set for January 27, 2009. Mr. Ferdon will continue to follow the matter. The Court also stated that the Estate was subject to two liens, one for DHS and one for Mr. Handlos' fees and costs.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-17-2008 Continued To Dec-23-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator. Michael Handlos for Clarence Bryant.

Mr. Handlos stated that monies needed to be collected and that administration expenses needed to be paid before the Petition For Final Distribution could be done. Mr. Ferdon noted that the Personal Representative was not present, stated that the home was in foreclosure, that the Public Administrator could not protect the home and that they would not take over without an accounting. In addition, Mr. Ferdon requested the status on the loan and any existing bank accounts. Mr. Handlos stated that he would attempt to get the accounting from his Client. The matter was continued to December 23, 2008. The Court will issue an order requesting Clarence Bryant to appear on December 23, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda./R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Aquino.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Nov-10-2008 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Handlos for Clarence Bryant.

Mr. Handlos stated that Mr. Bryant did not want to nominate his Cousin to serve as the Administrator and that he still wished to withdraw as Counsel. All matters were taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy S. Mann.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Handlos for Clarence Bryant (Administrator).

The matter was continued to August 26, 2008 for status. The Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel will be addressed at that time.

The Court granted the request for a continuance to May 19, 2008 for status of filing the Petition for final distribution.
